items.title: Microsoft Research's Mirage gives video generation a persistent spatial memory that doesn't forget what's around the corner
items.summary: Mirage, a video world model from Microsoft Research and several universities, stores scene information directly in latent space instead of pixel-based point clouds. That slashes compute time and graphics memory while keeping scenes spatially consistent through long camera moves. It still can't reliably track moving objects across segments. The article Microsoft Research's Mirage gives video generation a persistent spatial memory that doesn't forget what's around the corner appeared first on The Decoder.

items.title: Google Cloud's Open Knowledge Format turns scattered docs into Markdown files for AI agents
items.summary: Google Cloud's new Open Knowledge Format (OKF) standardizes scattered organizational knowledge as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter, making it portable and usable for AI agents. The minimalist spec formalizes a pattern Andrej Karpathy recently popularized as the "LLM Wiki." The article Google Cloud's Open Knowledge Format turns scattered docs into Markdown files for AI agents appeared first on The Decoder.

items.title: KPMG fabricated AI case studies in a report designed to sell clients on AI adoption
items.summary: KPMG published a report on AI in business that contained fabricated case studies involving UBS, the NHS, and other organizations. GPTZero CEO Edward Tian, who helped uncover the errors, warns of "secondary hallucinations," flawed claims from trusted consulting firms that spread unchecked. KPMG has since pulled the report. The article KPMG fabricated AI case studies in a report designed to sell clients on AI adoption appeared first on The Decoder.

items.title: AI coding agents find the right file but miss the exact lines that matter, study shows
items.summary: AI coding agents like Claude Code or Codex reliably find the right file but miss most of the critical lines within it. The new SWE-Explore benchmark is the first to test code search separately from the actual repair, and it shows that without enough context, even the best fix will fail. The article AI coding agents find the right file but miss the exact lines that matter, study shows appeared first on The Decoder.

items.title: Amazon and five other companies reportedly triggered the government crackdown on Anthropic's Fable model
items.summary: Amazon CEO Andy Jassy and executives from other tech companies reportedly warned the Trump administration about security vulnerabilities in Anthropic's Fable model, even though Amazon is one of Anthropic's largest investors. Within hours, the White House forced the model offline through an export control order. The move may well be a legitimate security policy decision, but it also reads as a show of force against an inconvenient company. The article Amazon and five other companies reportedly triggered the government crackdown on Anthropic's Fable model appeared first on The Decoder.

items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，根据多位网友今日晒出的截图，AI 版支付宝目前正在内测中，需要邀请码才可以体验。从曝光的界面可以看到，AI 版支付宝包含资产、阿宝两个版块，其中的“阿宝”就是一个 AI 助手界面，用户可以和阿宝对话，底部则是支付宝的扫一扫、收付款、出行、理财几大功能。而在资产界面，AI 版支付宝采用卡片夹形式展示用户的流动资产、理财资产、信用资产等信息。IT之家注意到，彭博社曝出蚂蚁集团正秘密测试 AI 版支付宝，消息称，新版本上线时间未定。对此，蚂蚁集团官方“拒绝评论”。另据 Tech 星球报道，AI 版支付宝已低调发出极少量测试邀请码。“这将是支付宝史诗级的改版”，一位抢先体验测试版的人士如是说。

items.title: 奔驰纯电 CLA 获推 OTA 升级：领航辅助驾驶系统覆盖全国城区及高速
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，梅赛德斯-奔驰官方今日宣布针对中国市场的奔驰纯电 CLA 推送新一轮 OTA 升级。本次更新聚焦智能辅助驾驶与数字座舱两个方面，共计带来 12 项功能新增与体验优化。据介绍，智能辅助驾驶是此次 OTA 的核心升级方向。奔驰与 Momenta 合作完成辅助驾驶系统技术升级，引入强化学习大模型，让车辆在复杂路口通行、邻车突然切入、弱势道路参与者防护等高频场景下的处理能力提升 5 至 10 倍，决策逻辑更贴近资深驾驶员。目前，城区与高速领航辅助驾驶功能已实现全国范围覆盖。IT之家附其他更新内容如下：新增 ETC 通行（提前识别闸机、ETC 通道类型与通行状态）优化车速调节更灵活新增泊出方向选择新增基于用户的辅助驾驶培训人优化人机共驾支持紧急车道保持优化车道内静态物体避让优化无导航红绿灯启停优化无导航自动变道除驾驶层面外，奔驰本次 OTA 重点升级 AI 数字座舱系统。全新虚拟助手“小奔”正式上线，该助手整合自然语言模型、3D 图形技术与拟人化语音系统，配备专属卡通形象与动态效果。除基础语音控制、信息查询功能外，“小奔”可识别用户情绪并做出互动反馈，还能联动车内灯光、音响、空调、座椅等硬件，根据语音指令一键营造不同座舱氛围。

items.title: 鸿海与施耐德电气围绕次世代 AI 数据中心展开战略合作，合作生产年内启动
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，鸿海科技集团 (Foxconn) 与施耐德电气 (Schneider Electric) 双方今日宣布展开战略合作，共同打造并推动次世代 AI 数据中心的发展与规模化应用。双方合作生产将于今年稍晚开始。IT之家了解到，本次合作中双方将结合鸿海的制造优势和施耐德电气的能源智慧，共同打造集成式、可实时部署的解决方案，协助客户在全球各地以更快的速度、更好的效率与更佳的预先规划，构建并运营 AI 基础设施。透过本次合作，鸿海与施耐德电气将共同开发次世代 AI 数据中心参考架构。同时，双方也将探索闭环式能源优化、模块化电力与冷却系统，以及标准化设计框架等创新解决方案，打造具备可重复性与高性能的 AI 工厂蓝图，推动全球 AI 基础设施标准化与规模化发展。

items.title: 2 Tile + 4 HBM 设计：英特尔未能商业化的 Xe HP 服务器 GPU 现身网络
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，Intel（英特尔）此前大举重返独立显卡市场时曾提出过“四大微架构”战略，分别是低功耗优化的 Xe LP、独立游戏显卡优化的 Xe HPG、机器学习 / 媒体优化的 Xe HP、高性能计算 / 机器学习优化的 Xe HPC。Xe LP、Xe HPG、Xe HPC 均有产品公开对外发售；但也称 "Arctic Sound" 的 Xe HP 最终仅用于内部和少数合作伙伴的开发者生态系统构建，未能商业化。X 平台 Chips by their layers (@ChipsByLayers) 北京时间 14 日分享了一颗 Xe HP 服务器 GPU 样品的实物图片。可以看到这款内部代号 QVS8 的芯片采用 2 计算模块 + 4HBM 堆栈的物理设计，设定频率为 1GHz。根据英特尔公布和外部泄露的消息，Xe HP 每个计算模块的最大规模为 512EU，配备 HBM2E 内存，提供 1 Tile、2Tile、4 Tile 三种版本。

items.title: 英国政府官宣 16 岁以下未成年人社交媒体禁令，预计涵盖 YouTube、TikTok、IG、X 平台等
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，英国首相基尔 · 斯塔默今天宣布，将禁止社交媒体平台向 16 岁以下未成年人提供服务，让孩子们不再花大量时间刷屏，多进行现实世界的活动和游戏。英国政府表示，本次禁令将为未来几代人树立全新社会标准，并推动文化层面改革，让每个孩子都拥有“最佳人生起点”。具体来说，英国计划采用类似澳大利亚的监管模式。禁令将覆盖允许用户发布内容、使用算法推荐和允许用户互动的平台，预计将覆盖 Snapchat、TikTok、YouTube、Instagram、Facebook 和 X 平台。政府表示，即时通讯服务软件（IT之家注：IM）不会被纳入禁令，因此 WhatsApp、Signal 等平台预计将继续接纳未成年人。同时，英国政府为了应对网络风险，将禁止 16 岁以下用户使用直播、与陌生人直接交流等功能，不仅适用于社交媒体，还将扩展至网游等在线互动服务。该国政府号称，上述措施的严格程度超越全球所有国家。此外，为了避免孩子在年满 16 岁时突然获得所有访问权限，英国政府还计划对 16-17 岁的青少年启用部分保护功能，并研究宵禁、强制中断无限滚动等功能，适用对象可能扩大至 18 岁以下人士，更多细节预计将在 7 月公布。英国首相斯塔默表示：“家长希望孩子们安全快乐地成长，但网络世界让这一切变得十分困难。我亲耳听到无数家庭呼吁改变，我们将回应他们的诉求。我们将采取世界上最严厉的

items.title: Omdia 下调 2026 年显示面板出货数量预测至衰退 6%
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，机构 Omdia 在其今日新闻稿中下调了对 2026 年显示面板市场的展望，认为在需求大幅萎缩的背景下该市场面临更多负面因素，对按数量计出货的预期从此前的 -2% 调整至 -6%、按面积计出货也从 +6% 降至 +1%。▲ 图源：OmdiaOmdia 认为 2026 年电视面板需求预计将同比下降 2.0%；该机构还将对全年笔记本电脑显示面板的需求预测下降约 720 万台。上游组件价格飙升正迫使终端设备制造商必须在具有竞争力的定价与不断上涨的生产成本之间寻求平衡，这可能导致规格下调或利润率缩减。而如果新定价过高，那么消费者的购买意愿将遭遇持续遏制。Omdia 显示研究部门高级首席分析师 Ricky Park 表示：显示面板行业正在应对一场多重挑战的“完美风暴”。中东地区持续的地缘政治不稳定已经实质性地影响了整个供应链的成本结构，石油价格上涨、存储器元件成本攀升以及关税相关压力迫使制造商重新审视定价策略。这导致我们相较于此前预测，大幅下调了需求预期。参考Omdia: 2026 display demand downgraded to 6% unit decline as supply chain pressures intensify

items.title: 研究发现：超大质量黑洞周边可能孕育数百万颗行星
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，一组科学家有了惊人发现：星系中明亮且活动剧烈的区域 —— 活动星系核，由不断吞噬物质的超大质量黑洞提供能量，这里或许是数百万颗行星的诞生地。活动星系核亮度极高，其光芒往往能超过所在星系内所有恒星的总光度。当超大质量黑洞被海量气体与尘埃包裹时，就会形成活动星系核。这些物质会汇聚成扁平的盘状云团，也就是吸积盘，并围绕黑洞旋转。吸积盘中的一部分物质会逐渐被黑洞吞噬，其余物质则被导向黑洞两极，随后以接近光速的高能等离子体喷流形式向外喷射。中心超大质量黑洞的质量可达太阳的数百万倍乃至数十亿倍，其巨大引力会让吸积盘内的气体和尘埃产生剧烈摩擦，使这些物质在整个电磁波谱范围内发出耀眼光芒。这一发现出人意料。尽管活动星系核遍布气体和尘埃，而二者正是构成行星的基础物质，但吸积盘内部环境动荡，通常并不被认为适合行星形成。不过，吸积盘边缘的温度与环境，和新生恒星周围孕育行星的原行星盘十分相似。久而久之，这里的尘埃能否不断聚集，最终演化成行星？为验证这一猜想，科学家构建了超大质量黑洞及其吸积盘的计算机模型，并录入吸积盘边缘的环境数据，模拟数百万年间尘埃的聚集速度以及原始行星的成长过程。科罗拉多大学博尔德分校的研究人员、团队成员布彭德拉・米什拉在接受 Space.com 采访时表示：“我们发现，在距离活动星系核内超大质量黑洞数十秒差距（1 秒差距约合 3.3 光年）的区域，能够

items.title: 德国数字部长卡斯滕 · 维尔德贝格被指多篇文章依赖 AI
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，根据德国媒体《DIE ZEIT》利用 AI 检测工具 Pangram 的分析，该国联邦数字化与国家现代化部部长卡斯滕 · 维尔德贝格的多篇文章存在依赖 AI 的迹象。图源：Pixabay这些内容既有署名以维尔德贝格的客座文章，也有演讲稿，甚至包括其在德国国会的发言；典型表现是高频使用破折号、排比 / 并列、否定 / 反诘。统计显示该部长的 30 篇演讲稿中有 1 篇完全由 AI 生成，另外 7 篇则得到 AI 辅助。有趣的是，被认为利用 AI 的文章提到了警惕对外国 AI 依赖、提醒 AI 危险等内容。德国联邦数字化与国家现代化部发言人表示，该部部长将 AI 视为一种工具，承认部分内容起草得到 AI 辅助，但人类始终必须为审查和验证工作负责。

items.title: KENWOOD 推出 GLASS Core 系列：业界首款玻璃振膜无线耳机
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，JVC（杰伟世）旗下 JVCKENWOOD（杰伟世建伍）即将在 12 月迎来诞生 60 周年。作为 60 年纪念产品的开端，KENWOOD 品牌日本当地时间本月 11 日宣布推出业界首款应用玻璃振膜的无线耳机 —— GLASS Core 系列。其中 Pro 款还创新性地将玻璃振膜与 MEMS 驱动器相结合，在高低频段均带来通透清晰的表现。▲ GLASS Core ProKENWOOD 表示，玻璃振膜凭借其宽广的频率范围与声场、清晰的音质、强劲的冲击力能释放出令人心潮澎湃的清澈音调，营造出身临其境的现场感。▲ GLASS Core这两款耳机搭载液态硅胶耳塞，获得 Hi·Res AUDIO WIRELESS 认证，支持 LDAC 编解码器，搭载高性能降噪芯片；Pro 款还支持个性化音频、自研空间音频、自研 K2 无线音频质量重建技术。KENWOOD GLASS Core 续航 44hr，税后售价 27830 日元；GLASS Core Pro 续航 47hr，税后售价 49940 日元（IT之家注：现汇率约合 1177 / 2112 元人民币）。

items.title: 消息称字节跳动正与天数智芯洽谈购买 AI 芯片
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，据路透社今日援引知情人士消息，字节跳动正与总部位于上海的天数智芯（Iluvatar CoreX）洽谈，欲购买 AI 推理芯片，同时也在考虑与百度达成类似合作。据悉，如果本次交易达成，那么天数智芯将成为字节跳动第三家国产 GPU 主要供应商，排在华为和寒武纪之后。其中一名消息人士透露，字节跳动也在考虑百度旗下的“昆仑芯（IT之家注：Kunlunxin）”芯片。由于相关谈判尚未公开，相关人士要求匿名。作为领先的国产 GPU 创业公司之一，天数智芯预计将于今年向字节跳动供应至少 5 万颗芯片，其中绝大部分将用于 AI 推理任务，以支持豆包用户规模不断扩张。对于天数智芯而言，如果能获得字节跳动这种大型企业的订单，那么该公司的商业化进程将明显加快。在此之前，这家公司主要依靠政府采购项目。不过，目前潜在的交易细节仍未敲定，未来仍有可能发生变数。字节跳动、天数智芯、百度和腾讯均未回应置评请求。

items.title: 小米单联瑜：从今年下半年开始会有更多的品牌加强抽真空监管，部分品牌会采用「数字抽真空技术」
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，小米集团大家电部总经理单联瑜今日发文，谈及了小米空调在 2026 年做的三件非常重要的事情：超高 APF，对用户省电省钱，对社会节能减排，并且技术具备前瞻性，因为「超一级能效，大概率是符合下一代能效标准的产品系列」强劲风系列，纯热泵制热，用户体验不干燥，相比 PTC 电辅热技术更节能、更绿色，完美适配华南和长江流域数字抽真空，用互联网 +AI 的方式解决消费者的困扰，把很难监管的服务过程透明化单联瑜表示，自己有两个判断：从 27 年开始，一定会有更多的品牌和产品系列采用「纯热泵技术」从 26 年下半年开始，一定会有更多的品牌加强抽真空的监管，部分品牌会采用「数字抽真空技术」据IT之家此前报道，今年 5 月，小米公司发布内部通报，针对在数字化抽真空服务专项稽核中发现的两起空调安装抽真空造假事件作出顶格处理。根据网上流传出的公告截图，涉事的两名合作工程师被直接永久拉黑清退，终止任何形式的合作；其所属线下安装网点按虚假服务论处，每单罚款 1000 元，同时面向全国所有家装服务商同步通报批评，后续若再出现同类问题，将进一步升级追责力度。相关阅读：《小米通报两起空调安装抽真空造假事件：涉事工程师永久拉黑》

items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，比亚迪腾势今日宣布腾势 N9 迎来自 2025 年上市以来第 7 次升级，版本号为 V1.7.0，不含腾势 N9 闪充版。IT之家整理如下：新增加速踏板防误踩功能：车辆起步或低速行驶时猛踩加速踏板，系统将智能判断是否误踩，如果存在碰撞风险，将抑制车辆加速并报警提示。新增易三方灵动泊车功能：智能识别用户泊车意图，弹窗显示，无需复杂操作，一键触达易三方泊车。新增横风稳定护航功能：基于易三方强大的融合感知能力，瞬间识别横风，毫秒级扭矩调节，后轮转向协同，实现精准的横摆控制。新增防甩尾安全控制功能：实时检测车身姿态数据，易三方瞬时抑制车身横摆，主动补偿，化险于未然高速避险游刃有余。新增超级童锁功能：告别机械操作，一键锁止独立控制儿童侧。IoT 再添新成员：新增六大生态伙伴，覆盖出行全场景。新增车家互联智慧伙伴：新增海信及 TCL 品牌接入，支持远程控制家中空调。新增电动门控制便捷入口：屏幕底部可加选电动门控制按钮，实现零层级电动门，易识别、易操作，一键控制开启 / 关闭车门。QQ 音乐新增银河音效，酷狗音乐新增蝰蛇音效新增爱奇艺跨屏点播：长按影片封面，精彩即可共享，还可控制其他屏播放进度。百变主题新增时光音乐壁纸：感知天气和时间，调节屏幕颜色并为你推荐符合心境的音乐。新增 QQ 音乐「AI 陪你听」功能

items.title: 科幻作家郝景芳承认新书的 AI 写作比重占到一半，引发网络争议
items.summary: IT之家 6 月 15 日消息，根据深圳龙岗融媒 6 月 5 日报道，雨果奖获奖作品《北京折叠》的作者郝景芳在接受采访时透露，在她今年新出的小说《银河学院》里，AI 写作的比重已经占到一半了。报道中还提到，郝景芳表示“出版社的编辑还一个劲夸我今年写得好”，并称其实读者也看不出来哪些部分是 AI 写的。该报道发布后在网络上引起了不小的争议，相关话题今天也登上微博热搜。有网友认为“作家放弃了自己的笔”，还有网友表示承认用 AI 写作是迈出了一大步。而龙岗融媒的这篇报道目前已经无法查到。IT之家查询获悉，《银河学院》是郝景芳创作的少儿科幻小说系列，她今年 3 月就在微博中就提到了自己搭建的 AI 辅助创意写作工作台。

items.summary: 36氪获悉，恒指收涨0.5%，恒生科技指数涨1.28%；贵金属、半导体、零售板块领涨，智谱涨超32%，紫金黄金国际涨超15%，兆易创新涨超10%，国美零售涨超9%；能源、银行、食品饮料板块走弱，山东墨龙跌超9%，中原银行跌超4%，百威亚太跌超2%；南向资金净买入21.1亿港元。

items.title: 机构：千问AI眼镜1-5月全国销量第一
items.summary: 6月15日，根据华信人咨询出具的市场销量数据，2026年1-5月期间，千问AI眼镜在AI眼镜品类中销量位居全国第一。此前，奥维云网（AVC）线上零售监测数据显示，2026年3月至4月，千问AI眼镜全系列（含夸克AI眼镜）线上市场零售额份额达30.4%，接近整体市场的三分之一，位居线上市场第一。据了解，7月千问AI眼镜还将迎来新一轮升级发布，进一步拓展AI眼镜在日常生活和工作场景中的应用体验。

items.title: A Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL)-Based Transformer Method for Solving the Open Shop Scheduling Problem
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13682v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The open shop scheduling problem (OSSP) arises in many industrial and service settings but remains computationally challenging as the number of jobs and machines increases. While exact methods quickly become intractable, classical dispatching rules and metaheuristics may require substantial tuning to maintain solution quality at large scales. This study develops a Transformer-based scheduling policy for OSSP using an encoder-decoder architecture with multi-head attention. items.title: UP-NRPA: User Portrait based Nested Rollout Policy Adaptation for Planning with Large Language Models in Goal-oriented Dialogue Systems
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13683v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: To address the challenge that current dialogue policy planning methods struggle to dynamically adapt to diverse user characteristics, this paper proposes a User Portrait based Nested Rollout Policy Adaptation (UP-NRPA) online framework with Large Language Models. In contrast to conventional approaches dependent on model training and require offline reinforcement learning policy models for user groups, UP-NRPA enables dynamic customization of dialogue strategies through an adaptive mechanism. items.title: History of the Muddy Children Puzzle
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13703v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Muddy Children Puzzle is a puzzle about knowledge and ignorance that has been inspiring for the development of epistemic logic. Who came up with it first? This is unclear. We trace the origin of the Muddy Children Puzzle through logical and literary publications over the past two centuries. The puzzle inspired a numerous variations such as involving numbers or coloured hats. We also present a novel hats puzzle involving self-reference.

items.title: Orchestra-o1: Omnimodal Agent Orchestration
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13707v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The recent success of agent swarms has shifted the paradigm of large language model (LLM)-based agents from single-agent workflows to multi-agent systems, highlighting the importance of agent orchestration for task decomposition and collaboration. However, existing orchestration frameworks are limited to a narrow set of modalities and struggle to generalize to more complex settings where heterogeneous modalities coexist and interact. items.title: Hybrid Open-Ended Tri-Evolution Makes Better Deep Researcher
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13710v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deep research and agent evolution serve as de-facto tasks for AI agents in real-world applications toward artificial general intelligence. The former enables autonomous retrieval and integration of information in open-ended environments to tackle open-ended research tasks, yet it is constrained by the static parametric deep research capabilities of agent systems. items.title: WorkBench Revisited: Workplace Agents Two Years On
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13715v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The best agent on WorkBench in March 2024, GPT-4, completed 43% of tasks and took an unintended harmful action, such as emailing the wrong person, on 26% of them. We re-visit the benchmark in June 2026 and find that the best agent to date, Claude Opus 4.8, completes 89% and takes an unintended harmful action on 2.5%. items.title: Refusal Beyond a Single Direction: A Preliminary Comparison of Diff-in-Means and INLP
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13720v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Arditi et al. (2024) has shown that refusal in safety fine-tuned chat models is mediated by a single linear direction in the residual stream, recoverable by a difference-in-means (DiM) of harmful and harmless activations. items.title: YeasierAgent: Agentic Social Sandbox as a Canvas for Intent-Driven Creation of Platform-Agnostic Symbiotic Agent-Native Applications
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13722v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper introduces YeasierAgent, an application-building paradigm based on symbiotic agents, narrative worlds, and scene-aware interaction. It challenges the conventional device-coupled model of software by redefining applications as collaborative spaces among users, agents, and worlds. items.title: TwinBI: An Agentic Digital Twin for Efficient Augmented Interactions with Business Intelligence Dashboards
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13731v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Business intelligence (BI) increasingly combines dashboard interaction with LLM-based assistance, but these two modes often fall out of sync during multi-step analysis. As users switch between direct dashboard manipulation and natural-language queries, it becomes difficult to preserve a consistent analytical state across filters, hierarchies, metrics, and chart context. items.title: When Sample Selection Bias Precipitates Model Collapse
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13732v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The proliferation of recursive training on synthetic data can alleviate data scarcity but risks model collapse, where repeated training erodes distributional tails and homogenizes outputs. Data selection is widely viewed as a remedy, yet its reliability depends critically on the reference distribution used by the verifier. items.title: AI Receptivity or AI Adoption Breadth? A Tool-Specific Reanalysis of the Lower-Literacy/Higher-Usage Link
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13734v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent evidence reported by Tully, Longoni, and Appel (2025) suggests that lower artificial intelligence (AI) literacy predicts greater receptivity toward AI. We revisit this claim using the public data from Study 3 of that article, which measures past usage of five AI tool categories on a five-point frequency scale. items.title: MA-ProofBench: A Two-Tiered Evaluation of LLMs for Theorem Proving in Mathematical Analysis
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13782v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have made notable progress in automated theorem proving, yet existing formal benchmarks remain limited in both mathematical coverage and difficulty. Most are concentrated in areas that are easier to formalize, such as algebra and elementary number theory, and provide limited coverage of subfields that require deeper reasoning, including mathematical analysis. items.title: Poker Arena: Multi-Axis Profiling of Strategic Reasoning and Memory in LLMs
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13815v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Strategic reasoning under uncertainty underpins consequential decisions in negotiation, finance, and policy, but prevailing game-play benchmarks collapse heterogeneous reasoning dimensions into a single scalar, leaving the capability structure of frontier LLMs unexamined. items.title: Hyperdimensional computing for structured querying on tabular data embeddings
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13871v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tabular data embeddings have become a cornerstone of data profiling and data integration pipelines, enabling tasks such as entity annotation and resolution; schema matching; column type detection; and table search, among others. items.title: Capability Minimization as a Safety Primitive: Risk-Aware Causal Gating for Least-Privilege LLM Agents
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13884v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern decision systems increasingly rely on learned components whose outputs may be confident yet wrong, exposing downstream actions to costly errors. We introduce Risk-Aware Causal Gating (RACG), a framework that decides whether to act on, defer, or abstain from a model's prediction by combining causal effect estimation with calibrated risk control. items.title: A Multi-Agent AI System for Automated High School Transcript Processing: Collaborative Document Analysis at Scale
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13916v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Each year, college admissions offices face an overwhelming challenge: processing millions of high school transcripts, each with unique formats, grading systems, and layouts. This manual process creates operational bottlenecks that delay admissions decisions and consume valuable resources. items.title: Sorries Are Not the Hard Part: An Expert-Review Case Study of a Semi-Autonomous Formalization
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13925v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models can often close proof gaps in interactive theorem provers, but a verified theorem is not the same thing as a reusable library contribution. We study this distinction through a detailed case study: a semi-autonomous formalization of Grothendieck's vanishing theorem. items.title: Adversarial Concept Search: Predicting Compositional Errors From Feature Geometry
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13934v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Humans cannot always intuit what scenarios are most challenging to LLMs. Hoping to capture challenging edge cases, developers either design problems to be difficult for humans or curate extensive benchmarks. What if we could instead anticipate which scenarios a model will fail on? In this paper, we use an LLM's representational geometry to predict which concept combinations it will fail on. items.title: Minim: Privacy-Aware Minimal View for Agents via Trusted Local Sanitization
items.summary: arXiv:2606.13949v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern LLM-powered autonomous agents increasingly rely on rich user interface (UI) state observations to achieve reliable action grounding in complex digital environments. However, many deployments transmit the full UI state to remote inference servers even when most elements are irrelevant to the current task, which can leak sensitive but unnecessary context such as authentication codes, private notifications, and background application states. items.title: Formalizing Numerical Analysis: An Agent Pipeline and Quality Audit Beyond Kernel Acceptance
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14000v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work has demonstrated that coding agents can formalize entire advanced mathematics textbooks in Lean 4, yet existing efforts concentrate on branches of mathematics already well-represented in mathlib and measure success solely through kernel acceptance. items.title: Applicability Condition Extraction for Therapeutic Drug-Disease Relations
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14031v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Identifying conditions that a certain drug takes therapeutic effect on a target disease is crucial for clinical decision-making support. However, most existing biomedical information extraction methods have focused on identifying only relations between drugs and diseases, while largely overlooking the context-specific conditions where such relations can apply. items.title: FactoryLLM: A Safe and Open-Source AI Playground for Evaluating LLMs in Smart Factories
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14119v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fault diagnostics and recovery in smart factories is challenging because critical information is dispersed across manuals of multiple machines which are interconnected through the manufacturing process. Large Language Models (LLMs) can provide a promising approach. items.title: VeriGeo: Controllable Geometry Question Generation with Numerical and Analytical Verification
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14176v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Geometry problem generation is useful for AI-assisted education and multimodal mathematical reasoning, but reliable synthesis remains difficult because the problem statement, diagram, constraints, and solution should be mutually consistent. Existing methods often trade off controllability and reliability: seed-based rewriting is flexible but weakly verifiable, whereas diagram-first construction improves validity but is less suited to arbitrary user-specified constraints. items.title: When Should Agent Trust Be Conditional? Characterizing and Attacking Skill-Conditional Reputation in Agent Swarms
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14200v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Open platforms increasingly route tasks among heterogeneous LLM agents--differing in base model, scaffold, and tool stack--whose competence varies sharply by skill: an agent excellent at one skill may be useless at another. The standard reputation approach summarizes each agent by a single global trust score, but that scalar is the wrong object here, because routing every task to the globally most-trusted agent leaves the value of specialization unclaimed. items.title: Closing the Reflection Gap: A Free Calibration Bonus for Agentic RL
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14211v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLMs are increasingly deployed as agents that interact with external environments and observe feedback such as execution results, error messages, and tool outputs. A well-functioning agent should be able to leverage this feedback to accurately assess its own performance. items.title: SkillAudit: Ground-Truth-Free Skill Evolution via Paired Trajectory Auditing
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Agent skills are structured procedural packages that guide frozen LLM agents in specialized workflows. Skills rarely remain sufficient after deployment: edge cases, API changes, and deployment constraints become visible only through use, making skill evolution a practical necessity. items.title: AFFORDANCE20Q: Evaluating Affordance Reasoning from Physical Properties
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14240v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Affordance reasoning, the inference of an object's action possibilities from its physical properties (e.g., shape and material), is fundamental to human physical understanding and increasingly critical for Large Language Models (LLMs). items.title: HarnessX: A Composable, Adaptive, and Evolvable Agent Harness Foundry
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14249v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agent performance depends critically on the runtime harness, comprising the prompts, tools, memory, and control flow that mediate how a model observes, reasons, and acts. Yet today's harnesses remain largely hand-crafted and static: each new model or task still demands bespoke scaffolding, and the rich traces produced during execution are rarely distilled back into systematic improvement. items.title: Communication Policy Evolution for Proactive LLM Agents
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14314v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM agents have rapidly evolved into autonomous systems, yet a persistent information gap remains between users and agents: communication is costly, while users' identical preferences further limit information exchange. items.title: CSPO: Constraint-Sensitive Policy Optimization for Safe Reinforcement Learning
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14415v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Safe reinforcement learning (Safe RL) aims to maximize expected return while satisfying safety constraints, typically modeled as Constrained Markov Decision Processes (CMDPs). While primal-dual methods scale well to deep RL, they often suffer from delayed constraint correction, leading to oscillatory behavior and prolonged safety violations. items.title: Causal Object-Centric Models for Planning with Monte Carlo Tree Search
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14418v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce COMET (Causal Object-centric Model for Efficient Tree search), a model-based reinforcement learning algorithm that performs Monte Carlo Tree Search in a slot-structured latent space. items.title: GitOfThoughts: Version-Controlled Reasoning and Agent Memory You Can Replay, Diff, and Merge
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14470v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) reasoning is ephemeral: chains of thought vanish with the context window, pruned search branches leave no record, and memory buffers cannot be diffed, merged, or audited. items.summary: arXiv:2606.14476v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A growing line of work equips large language model (LLM) agents with graph neural networks (GNNs) as callable tools, assuming the agent exercises judgment over when and how much to rely on such a tool. items.title: From Chatbot to Digital Colleague: The Paradigm Shift Toward Persistent Autonomous AI
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14502v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) are undergoing a fundamental transformation from conversational generators into integrated AI systems capable of reasoning, action, memory, and self-improvement. items.title: Dense Coordinate-List Fine-Tuning Induces a Controllable Interference Surface in Vision-Language Models
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14507v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fine-tuning vision-language models to emit dense coordinate lists improves visual grounding but also changes how models serialize, repeat, and terminate structured outputs. items.title: Every Eval Ever: A Unifying Schema and Community Repository for AI Evaluation Results
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14516v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI evaluations are widely used for testing and understanding progress. First, results are saved in incompatible formats, scattered across leaderboards, papers, blog posts, evaluation harness logs, and custom repositories. items.title: StreamMemBench: Streaming Evaluation of Agent Memory for Future-Oriented Assistance
items.summary: arXiv:2606.14571v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A central role of personal-agent memory is to turn stored information and prior interactions into future-oriented assistance. items.summary: arXiv:2606.14579v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: When applying Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) for GUI Grounding, rollouts are sampled from a single screenshot view; groups often become either all failures on difficult instances or all successes on easy ones, yielding no useful relative advantage. items.summary: arXiv:2606.14582v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Efficient route optimization play a vital role in ensuring both safety and punctuality in railway operations. items.summary: arXiv:2606.14654v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sequential or time-stamped interaction logs provide objective records of digital application usage, yet their granularity and noise often obscure meaningful insights into people's work.
